reasoning: Structural divergence is real and held under attack. OUR side (sim.ts:1184-1189): spawnTie indexes SPAWN_LATERALS[spawnIndex % 12] — a flat 12-entry table walked monotonically 1A1->1A2->...->1D3. The counter GameState.spawnCount increments by exactly 1 on each spawn (sim.ts:213-214) and is NEVER reset on phase/wave entry — enterPhase (sim.ts:963-1007) resets phaseKills/enemies/trench state but not spawnCount — so the lateral walk is fully monotonic across the whole game and wave-INDEPENDENT, with NO tie between a lateral slot and any maneuver (our sim has no choreography at all). ROM side (WSCPU.MAC:1241-1276): the .WV macro emits a TRIPLE `.WORD TS.shape / .WORD TCHchoreo / .WORD TBGbeginloc`, so every begin-location IS paired with a specific choreography script (e.g. TWV1A: .WV TIE,1A1,1A1 pairs choreography 1A1 with begin-loc 1A1). Waves are assembled from grouped TWV entries selected through the TSPWAV SET table (WSCPU.MAC:1218-1235). The headline claim ('spawn walks all 12 slots monotonically; the ROM pairs 3-of-a-group with fixed choreography') is TRUE. CONFIRMED, but two sub-claims in the reasoning are wrong — see corrections; they do not overturn the divergence, only its explanation.
corrections: FALSE SUB-CLAIM: the reasoning states 'our walk visits the ±2048 D-corners on 3 of every 12 spawns in every wave, whereas the ROM only presents ±2048 when a D-group wave/level is active.' The ROM DOES present the ±2048 D-corners in every wave. TWV2Z (WSCPU.MAC:1299-1320) uses begin-locations 1D1/1D2/1D3 nine times, and TWV2Z is the sustaining group in EVERY space-wave set (SETA1..SETA6, WSCPU.MAC:1230-1235 — SET A1 = TWV1A,TWV2B,TWV2C,TWV2Z is the first wave). So both ours and the ROM show ±2048 corners in the first wave; the asserted per-wave lateral-DISTRIBUTION gap ('ROM shows no D-corners early') is illusory. The genuine divergence is the missing begin-loc<->choreography pairing and the TSPWAV SET structure/order, NOT a ±2048-only-in-D-waves distribution difference.

IMPRECISE: 'a wave draws only ONE group's 3 begin-locations' — a space wave is a TSPWAV SET that CHAINS several TWV groups (SET A1 chains TWV1A,TWV2B,TWV2C,TWV2Z), so a wave draws several groups' begin-locations, and the per-wave lateral set is the union of those groups (which, via TWV2Z, always includes the D-corners).

reasoning: Verified ALL exit paths out of the space phase, not just the cited line. PHESP1 (WSMAIN.MAC:1415-1455) increments PH.TIM every game frame (LDD PH.TIM / ADDD #1 / STD PH.TIM) and drives a MUSIC sequence off PH.TIM thresholds: ==40 theme (PMTH5/PMDAR), ==200 PMTHB, ==400 PMDES, then >=420 sets `LDA #PH$SP2 ;GO AHEAD AND DESCEND` (WSMAIN.MAC:1441-1444, CMPD #2+7+1+9+1+1*20. IFHS — MACRO-11 left-to-right eval = 21*20. = 420 decimal frames). Throughout, it keeps <=3 aliens alive and refills on death (LDA WV.LIV / CMPA #03 / IFLO / JSR ADASHP, :1450-1454). The ONLY other PHASE write in the space phase is PHESP2->PH$S0G (WSMAIN.MAC:1490), taken after CPURET makes survivors 'run away' and ALL alien A$TYP slots read 0 — a retreat/empty test, not a kill quota. There is NO kill-count gate anywhere: killing more TIEs never ends the wave sooner, killing none still descends on schedule. OURS gates space clear on s.phaseKills >= SPACE_WAVE_QUOTA (=6) (state.ts:501, sim.ts:843). Genuine divergence. (Exact PH.TIM schedule is pair-timing's to ratify; the finding correctly defers it.)

Attacked the 'was 30 a correct half-of-60 for some value?' angle and it fails — 30 is wrong at every use site. TICK_HZ has exactly TWO consumers, both feeding ROM per-game-frame quantities: (1) ENEMY_SHOT_TTL = 64 / TICK_HZ (state.ts:230), the ROM 64-frame fireball life; (2) homing decay `Math.pow(7/8, dt * TICK_HZ)` (sim.ts:1081), the ROM 7/8-per-frame homing law. Both are decremented/applied once per game frame in the ROM mainline (MVGUN-class shot movement), and ground truth pins the game frame at 20.508 Hz (WSINT.MAC:145-148). So both run 30/20.508 = 1.463x too fast: the 64-tick life is 64/30 = 2.13 s vs the correct 64/20.508 = 3.12 s (0.68x), and the homing decays 1.46x too fast in wall-clock. ENEMY_SPEED is explicitly NOT routed through TICK_HZ (units/sec via pos += vel*dt, state.ts:206) — so no consumer is accidentally correct at 30. The frame-rate-INDEPENDENT structure of both laws is correct (pow composes); only the shared BASIS constant is wrong. state.ts:214-216's justification ('NOT pinned by the disassembly ... PROVISIONAL') is refuted by the MACRO-11 source, which pins it. Arithmetic verified. Confirmed; the finding correctly scopes itself to the BASIS and defers the fireball feel re-tune to pair-guns/pair-tie-ai.

trenchTimer is a per-STEP counter, not dt-scaled: `const trenchTimer = state.trenchTimer + 1` (sim.ts:609), advanced once per stepGame call. Verified the step cadence end-to-end: main.ts:136-271 calls createLoop with NO third argument, and @arcade/shared/dist/loop.js:39-40 defaults `hz = 60` -> `dt = 1/60`, a FIXED 60 Hz timestep — so stepGame runs 60x/second and trenchTimer counts 60/second. The ROM voice-timer word_4B0E advances once per game frame (phase execute handler dispatched once per WAITFRAME game frame, WSMAIN.MAC:306-314), i.e. 20.508/second. 60/20.508 = 2.926x too fast. Confirmed nothing downstream rescales it: trenchTimer is only compared to fixed integer thresholds in TRENCH_VOICE_CUES (16 even / 22 odd / 24 even, sim.ts:914-916). So cues fire at 16/60=0.27 s .. 24/60=0.40 s instead of the ROM's 16/20.508=0.78 s .. 24/20.508=1.17 s, bunching into the first ~0.4 s of the ~4.8 s (2400/500) trench. Arithmetic verified. Confirmed.

Re-derived every step from source. ROM forward speed M$VX = $300 (hex; radix 16) = 768 units, set at trench entry PHIBS (WSMAIN.MAC:1834-1835: LDD #300 / STD M$VX+M.S1, 'INITIAL PLAYER SPEED'). Integrated once per game frame by S1MVBS (WSMAIN.MAC:2654-2657: LDD M$VX+M.S1 / ADDD M$TX+M.S1 / STD M$TX+M.S1). Verified $300 is a per-GAME-FRAME velocity: S1MVBS has a SINGLE caller (WSMAIN.MAC:1856, inside PHEBS, the trench execute handler dispatched once per game frame), and it NEVER rewrites M$VX (reads it at :2655/:2660/:2685; stores M$TX/M$TY/M$TZ/M$VY/M$VZ but never M$VX) -> constant $300/frame in the steady trench (the SMVG9B (300-M$VX)/8 ramp at :2629-2633 is the ground->base TRANSITION, before the trench proper). So 768 x 20.508 Hz = 15,750 u/s. OURS: TRENCH_SCROLL_SPEED = 500 u/s (state.ts:401) -> 15750/500 = 31.5x too slow. Geometry-1:1 premise verified directly: trench-channel.ts places ribs at z = -k*RIB_Z + (scroll mod RIB_Z) with RIB_Z=2048 raw ROM units (sim advances scroll, port and obstacles by the SAME TRENCH_SCROLL_SPEED*dt), all projected uniformly — no hidden render Z-scale, so the ratio is render-scale-independent: a 2048-unit rib passes in 2048/500 = 4.1 s vs ROM 2048/15750 = 0.13 s. Both the closing speed and the geometry are relative (pilot moves through static walls in ROM; walls scroll past a static pilot in ours) — apples-to-apples. Arithmetic verified. Confirmed, no unit error found.

ROM trench has a real, data-driven LENGTH and a dynamic port location; ours is a lone fixed port. ROM (WSBASE.MAC:1014-1160): NWBASE picks a pie (TPIE predefined for base-star waves, RPIE random pie past the table), then NWFAR/DOFAR chain wedges of $800 (SHORT) or $1000 (LONG); IWEDGE records the END wall at BS.ELC when it reaches a TYP$END wedge (:1146-1151, STD BS.ELC 'SETS END LOCATION') and the port at BS.PLC when it reaches a TYP$PORT wedge (:1156-1159, STD BS.PLC 'LOCATION OF THE PORT') — so both the port distance and the trench end are DYNAMIC, set by where those wedges fall in the chain, downrange within a streaming window. OURS (sim.ts:1018): spawnPort() returns a single fixed [0,0,-EXHAUST_PORT_DISTANCE] = [0,0,-2400]; there is no channel body, no end wall, no length, and on a miss the port is simply re-spawned at the same fixed 2400 (sim.ts:794, 'another pass down the trench'). Genuine structural shortfall (position/length values are frame-invariant, so no unit artifact). Confirmed. Minor note below.
corrections: The length ESTIMATES are approximate, not pinned: 'a full pie is 16 wedges -> on the order of $8000-$10000+ units' and 'our 2400 is ~1/4 of even one short-wedge pie' are not certified constants. The $8000 in DOFAR ('?WILL ALL OF NEW WEDGES FIT INTO 8000?', WSBASE.MAC:1111) is a streaming look-ahead WINDOW governing how many wedges are generated ahead of the player, not the certified total trench length. The structural divergence (variable data-driven wedge chain with dynamic BS.PLC port + BS.ELC end wall vs a lone fixed 2400 port) stands independent of the exact length figure.
